channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Three Indian-Inspired Ways to Turn Eggs into a Satisfying Main Meal
item.description: Australian food writer Sarina Kamini explores three inventive Indian-inspired methods for transforming humble eggs into hearty, flavour-packed main meals in her latest Good Food feature. She explains ...

item.title: Four dead and several injured in two taxi collisions in Mpumalanga over Easter weekend
item.description: Four people lost their lives and many were injured in two unrelated taxi crashes that took place in Mpumalanga on Monday, marking a sombre end to the Easter long weekend. The first collision happened ...

item.title: BFP removes all NCR fire inspectors as part of corruption cleanup effort
item.description: The Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) has ordered the relief of all fire inspectors assigned in the National Capital Region (NCR) as part of a wide-ranging effort to eliminate corruption within its rank...

item.title: Team Rivers Wins 53 Medals on Day 5 of Para Games, Director Applauds Athletes' Efforts
item.description: The Para Games in Rivers have seen the state's team shine brightly on day 5, winning a total of 53 medals in various sporting events. Despite being first-time participants and having a late start in p...

item.title: Ondo State Wrestlers Dominate Women’s Categories at Diri Classics
item.description: Ondo State female wrestlers really show dem muscle for Day Six of the Fourth Governor Douye Diri Wrestling Classics wey happen for Yenagoa on Monday. Dem capture two gold medals from the four women fr...

item.title: 12-Year-Old Arrested Following Fatal School Assault in Los Angeles
item.description: In Los Angeles, a 12-year-old student has been arrested in connection with the death of classmate Khimberly Zavaleta Chuquipa, who was struck in the head with a metal water bottle. Khimberly, who was ...

item.title: Mombasa restricts heavy trucks on Nyali Bridge during peak traffic hours
item.description: The County Government of Mombasa has announced temporary restrictions on heavy commercial vehicles using Nyali Bridge and other major Northern Mainland roads during peak hours, beginning next week unt...

item.title: Seven Eritrean Players Reportedly Vanish in South Africa After AFCON Qualification
item.description: Eritrea's football team has made history by qualifying for the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) after defeating Eswatini 4-1 in the qualifiers. However, just days after their victory, seven of the E...

item.title: New Zealand's Opportunity to Lead in Responsible AI Governance
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is becoming an integral part of everyday life in New Zealand, with nearly 80% of the population having used AI tools in the past year. However, rising concerns about the i...

item.title: Wireless Festival director explains decision to include Kanye West amid controversy
item.description: The managing director of Wireless Festival, Melvin Benn, has defended the festival's choice to feature Kanye West as a headliner in July, despite widespread criticism. The announcement has led to spon...

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Claims of 27 Lakh Bengal Voters in Doubtful List Ahead of Assembly Polls
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has rejected appeals from the West Bengal government and TMC supporters seeking the inclusion of nearly 27 lakh voters whose claims were marked 'doubtful' during the special...

item.title: A Critical Examination of US and Israeli Foreign Policy and Its Global Impact
item.description: In 2002, George W. Bush introduced the term 'Axis of Evil,' framing the world in stark moral binaries with the United States as the defender of civilisation and its adversaries as embodiments of chaos...

item.title: Kenya Met Issues Warning on Rising Winds and Waves Along Kenyan Coast
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department has issued a seven-day marine forecast warning of rising winds and waves along the Kenyan coast. The forecast indicates that while conditions will remain calm and m...

item.title: Angel Reese joins Atlanta Dream following trade from Chicago Sky
item.description: The Atlanta Dream have acquired two-time WNBA All-Star Angel Reese from the Chicago Sky, sending first-round draft picks in 2027 and 2028 to Chicago and securing the right to swap second-round picks i...

item.title: Four men arrested in connection with deadly stabbing outside London nightclub
item.description: Four men have been arrested following a deadly stabbing outside a nightclub in Peckham, southeast London. The incident occurred in the early hours of Monday when a 26-year-old man was fatally stabbed ...

item.title: Governor Idris Reaffirms APC’s Stronghold in Kebbi Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Governor Nasir Idris of Kebbi State don talk say him go make sure say President Bola Tinubu and the All Progressives Congress (APC) go win well-well for the 2027 general election. He yarn this one aft...

item.title: US-Iran peace talks stall as Trump sets deadline over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tensions between the US and Iran continue to escalate as Iran rejects a temporary ceasefire proposed by the United States, insisting instead on a permanent resolution to the ongoing conflict. Iran com...

item.title: Ben Wallace warns of UK's vulnerability to Iranian missile threat amid delay to Defence Readiness Bill
item.description: Former Defence Secretary Ben Wallace has warned that the United Kingdom would currently be unable to defend itself against a missile attack from Iran, calling on the government to be transparent with ...

item.title: Over 900 drivers arrested for drunk driving during Easter weekend in South Africa
item.description: Over the Easter long weekend, South African law enforcement authorities made significant strides in curbing road incidents caused by drunk driving. Between Thursday and Saturday, a total of 934 motori...

item.title: House Committee on Justice subpoenas Ramil Madriaga as key witness in impeachment case against Vice President Sara Duterte
item.description: The House Committee on Justice has issued a subpoena to Ramil Madriaga, identified as the self-confessed bagman of Vice President Sara Duterte, requiring him to testify in the ongoing impeachment proc...
